---
title: "Emmanuel Hackman"
subtitle: "PhD candidate in computational mathematics"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.profile-card}
![](assets/img/profile.jpg){.profile-photo fig-alt="Portrait of Emmanuel Hackman"}

::: {.bio}
I am a PhD candidate at the **University of Southern Mississippi**, working at the
intersection of **numerical analysis**, **scientific machine learning**, and
**fluid dynamics**.

My dissertation develops **physics-informed neural networks (PINNs)** for the
3D incompressible **Navier–Stokes equations**, with applications to
**cerebrospinal fluid flow in Parkinson's disease**, material science, and
high-Reynolds-number CFD.

[Research](research/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-sm}
[Publications](publications/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}
[CV](cv/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}
[Email](mailto:emmanuelhackman825@gmail.com){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}
:::
:::

## Research interests

[Physics-Informed Neural Networks]{.tag}
[Navier–Stokes Equations]{.tag}
[Computational Fluid Dynamics]{.tag}
[Numerical Analysis]{.tag}
[Scientific Machine Learning]{.tag}
[JAX / PyTorch Autodiff]{.tag}
[Cerebrospinal Fluid Flow]{.tag}
[Parkinson's Disease Imaging]{.tag}

## What I am working on

I am building a reproducible research pipeline that benchmarks PINN-based solvers
against classical numerical baselines (projection methods, spectral solvers, RK4
time integration) on canonical problems — Taylor–Green, Kovasznay, Beltrami flow —
and then transfers the validated method to medical imaging data.

The headline near-term result is a **3D Navier–Stokes PINN validated against the
analytic Beltrami benchmark**, with side-by-side classical baselines on $L^2$
error, divergence, and wall-clock cost. See [Research](research/index.qmd) for
details.

## Recent

- **2026-05** — First experiment runs (Kovasznay AD-vs-FV) completed; pressure metric fix landed.
- **2026-05** — Dissertation research workspace scaffolded with full reproducibility checklist.
- **2026** — Coursework and qualifying exam preparation at USM.

---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
**Open to collaborations** on PINNs for incompressible flow, inverse problems in
4D-flow MRI, and physics-informed benchmarks. [Get in touch.](contact.qmd)
:::
